summary: requests redirected to a malicious target leaks Proxy-Authorization header to the redirect target
guidance: Exploitable when (a) the caller uses requests with a Proxy-Authorization header, and (b) the URL or proxy origin can be influenced by an attacker. Hard-coded URLs to trusted hosts are typically safe.

summary: urllib3 does not remove Authorization HTTP header on cross-origin redirect
guidance: Most Python code reaches urllib3 transitively via requests. Direct urllib3 usage with redirect=True and an explicit Authorization header is the exploitable shape.

summary: jinja2 sandbox escape via str.format_map allows arbitrary attribute access
guidance: Only exploitable when the template TEXT itself comes from untrusted input. Templates whose source is hard-coded in the program — even if the data passed in is attacker-controlled — are NOT exploitable.

summary: ReDoS via the urlize filter on adversarial input
guidance: Reachable only if (a) a template uses the urlize filter and (b) the filtered input is attacker-controlled.

summary: PyYAML yaml.load(..., Loader=FullLoader) allows arbitrary Python object construction in versions <5.3.1
guidance: Exploitable only if the program actually calls yaml.load on untrusted input. Projects that import pyyaml transitively but never call its loaders are not exposed. Move to yaml.safe_load regardless.

summary: PyYAML versions <5.4 mishandle ! and !! tags, allowing arbitrary code execution via yaml.full_load on untrusted input
guidance: Same shape as CVE-2020-1747: requires .load() with the unsafe loader on attacker-controlled YAML. yaml.safe_load is unaffected.

summary: urllib3 before 1.25.9 allows CRLF injection if the attacker controls the request method or path
guidance: Reachable only when an attacker can inject newline characters into the HTTP method or path string. requests-level callers are usually safe because requests pre-encodes path/method.

summary: pip earlier than 21.1 mishandles unicode separators in package names, enabling install of unintended packages
guidance: Exposure depends on whether the program builds pip invocations from user input (unusual).

summary: Mako template engine ReDoS in the renderer
guidance: Reachable only when templates or template lookups derive from untrusted text.

summary: certifi included TrustCor as a root certificate authority; the CA was distrusted by major browsers
guidance: Mostly informational. Upgrade certifi and rebuild any container images. Code-side mitigation is not possible.

summary: requests leaks Proxy-Authorization to destination on cross-origin redirect when an HTTPS proxy is used
guidance: Same shape as CVE-2018-18074 but with an HTTPS proxy. Safe if no Proxy-Authorization header is set.

summary: idna allows denial-of-service via crafted host names (CPU/memory exhaustion)
patterns: internationalized domain name from untrusted source
guidance: Reachable only when the host portion of a URL is attacker-controlled and reaches idna.encode/decode (typical for URL parsers operating on raw user input).
